name: Kubecost Kubernetes Cost API
what_it_does: Open-source Kubernetes cost monitoring and allocation platform with REST API — running in-cluster to provide real-time cost attribution by namespace, deployment, pod, and label, with savings recommendations and multi-cloud cost integration.
use_cases: Querying Kubernetes workload costs by namespace and label for team chargeback
Monitoring cost trends and anomalies for k8s workloads via REST API
Generating cost allocation reports for FinOps review via reporting API
Querying savings recommendations for rightsizing and idle resource elimination
Integrating Kubecost cost data into custom dashboards or Slack cost reports
not_for: Non-Kubernetes cost management (use Vantage or Cloudability for VMs/serverless)
Cloud-native cost management without Kubernetes focus
Simple cost reporting without workload-level attribution
best_when: A platform or FinOps team needs granular Kubernetes cost attribution by team/namespace with in-cluster deployment and API access for custom reporting.
avoid_when: You don't run Kubernetes, need multi-cloud non-k8s cost management, or prefer SaaS cost tools without in-cluster deployment.

auth.notes: API key for Kubecost Enterprise. Open-source version has no auth by default (in-cluster access only). RBAC for Kubernetes cluster access controls who can port-forward. Enterprise adds SSO (SAML/OIDC). API key in header for enterprise.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: open-source version, single cluster, 15-day retention
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise from $10K/year; includes multi-cluster, SSO, longer retention
pricing.notes: Open-source core (Apache 2.0) with Enterprise tier. Runs in your Kubernetes cluster — no external SaaS dependency for core features. AWS EKS integration for enhanced data. OpenCost (open standard) spun out by Kubecost. Acquired by Strawberry Fields Capital in 2024. Wide k8s community adoption.

performance.rate_limits: In-cluster deployment — rate limits depend on cluster resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for allocations, assets, savings, and cost models. In-cluster deployment — access via port-forward or ingress. No auth on open-source (rely on k8s RBAC). Good documentation. Data based on real-time k8s metrics — more accurate than cloud billing APIs. Allocation API is the core for chargeback.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All API calls are read-only aggregations — fully idempotent. Cost data is computed from metrics, not stored as mutable records.
known_agent_gotchas: Default deployment has no authentication — secure with ingress auth or network policy
Data retention limited to 15 days on free tier — historical analysis requires Enterprise
In-cluster deployment means API endpoint varies — not a SaaS URL
Cost data accuracy depends on cloud pricing config and real-time k8s metrics

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable (in-cluster by default unencrypted). Open-source version has no auth by default — critical security gap for production. Enterprise adds SSO. Self-hosted — data never leaves cluster. Apache 2.0 license. OpenCost standard for interoperability.
